question: [File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/PublishProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a null key into the `scenarios` map via Spring Boot environment variable binding (e.g., `HIERO_MIRROR_MONITOR_PUBLISH_SCENARIOS_[null]=...`) to trigger a NullPointerException in `scenarios.keySet().stream().anyMatch(StringUtils::isBlank)` before the blank-name guard fires, causing application startup failure (DoS)?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/PublishProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Field: batchDivisor] Can an unprivileged external user set `hiero.mirror.monitor.publish.batchDivisor` to exactly `100` (the minimum allowed by `@Min(100)`) combined with an extremely high total TPS across scenarios, causing `Math.ceil(total / batchDivisor)` to produce an enormous batch size that floods the network with transactions and causes resource exhaustion?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/PublishProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a scenario name of extreme length (e.g., 1 MB string) via configuration binding, causing `property.setName(name)` to store an oversized string that is then embedded in every log line and metric label, causing log/metric storage exhaustion?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/PublishProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Field: responseThreads] Can an unprivileged external user set `responseThreads` to `1` (minimum allowed by `@Min(1)`), causing all asynchronous response resolution to serialize on a single thread, creating a processing bottleneck that causes transaction timeouts and cascading failures in the publish flow?
